paradigm: turn-based tactics RPG with scenario-based narratives

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible enemy sprites on overworld that can be approached or avoided
danger_signals.meaning: Telegraphed threats allow player tactical positioning decisions

danger_signals.visual_form: Antagonist names incorporating 'Odio' across all scenarios (Odo, Ou Di Wan Lee, Ode Iou, O.D.)
danger_signals.meaning: Recurring thematic danger marker—unified antagonist presence across disparate timelines

danger_signals.visual_form: 7x7 grid battlefield with positioned characters and elemental tile effects
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards and area-of-effect abilities create spatial danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Stealth-based exposure, telepathy-based discovery, scripted vs. random encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Danger manifestation differs by era—prehistoric physical threat, feudal political intrigue, Wild West reputation/bounty systems

safety_signals.visual_form: Character groupings and collaborative story progression (Pogo + Beru + allied tribe members)
safety_signals.meaning: Tribal or faction belonging indicates protective social bonds

safety_signals.visual_form: Experience-based progression, story-locked character advancement, observed technique adoption
safety_signals.meaning: Completion of training arcs (Shifu lineage continuation) signals safety through knowledge acquisition

safety_signals.visual_form: Story milestones where protected targets (Beru in Prehistory, Sakamoto Ryōma in Edo) are extracted
safety_signals.meaning: Achieving protective objectives reduces narrative threat level

safety_signals.visual_form: Sparkling effect overlay on gatherable items (remake enhancement)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability signals environmental readiness and preparation capability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dinosaur god Odo as opposing faction marker
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rival tribe members vs. aligned tribe members (visual differentiation not specified)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Prehistoric cave/tribal settlement environments; sacrifice ritual locations

faction_indicators.faction: Earthen Heart Martial Art School (Imperial China)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rival school (led by Ou Di Wan Lee) as opposing martial tradition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ninja training hierarchy; daimyo political authority
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Demon-disguised daimyo (Ode Iou) as supernatural political threat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle interiors; political strongholds; shadow/infiltration spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gunslinger code of honor and dueling tradition
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Crazy Bunch bandit gang as outlaw opposition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cave environments, primitive tribal aesthetics, sacrifice ritual staging
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exiling of protagonists, forced ritual participation, tribal alliance-forming through combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dojo/training ground architecture, Shifu aging/weakening visual representation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Selective disciple mentorship, deliberate assassination of rival students, life-force sacrifice animations

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Edo Japan—political instability masked by demonic occupation
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle political interiors, disguised supernatural threat (demon daimyo visual markers unclear)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission-based infiltration, rescue of key political figures (Sakamoto Ryōma), honor-bound combat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Wild West—frontier lawlessness and bandit control
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isolated frontier town under gang occupation, outlaw/bounty hunter visual differentiation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dueling encounters, gang liberation arc, bounty hunter alliance formation

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Present day / Near future / Distant future scenarios

environmental_storytelling.example: Some scenarios use random encounters, others visible avoidable enemy sprites, others entirely scripted—each reflects era's threat prevalence and player agency
Pogo exiled after rescue; Oboromaru sent on dangerous infiltration—establishes personal stakes through environmental removal
Sacrifice ritual to dinosaur god Odo, dojo training grounds, castle political chambers—setting communicates cultural values and threat sources
Resource visibility signals era-appropriate survival mechanics and preparation necessity
Odio name/incorporation across all seven scenarios creates visual narrative cohesion despite temporal/setting isolation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Life-force depletion animation; transitional mentor-to-ancestral presence; power bestowal before mortality
Maintains false daimyo identity until confrontation; orchestrates political kidnapping; combat engagement triggers revelation
Active agency in saving Beru despite tribal punishment; tribe-unification through alliance-building and combat victory
Initial duel opponent becomes collaborative liberator; alliance formation signals world-state shift from lawlessness to collective action

notes: Live A Live employs scenario-based world-state manifestation—each era (prehistoric, imperial, feudal, frontier, modern, future) has distinct visual communication systems reflecting cultural threat models and social structures. The recurring Odio antagonist name serves as a unified narrative signal across disconnected timelines. Turn-based 7x7 grid combat with elemental tile effects creates spatial danger communication. The 2022 remake added visual clarity through sparkling item indicators, health/charge bars, and area-of-effect previews—modernizing the original's implicit danger signaling. Faction identity appears primarily through narrative protagonist grouping rather than explicit color/symbol systems in documented sources.
